summary: Prefix top-k bisect localizes the first hipEngine vs parent sequential argmax divergence to max_layers=2, i.e. after adding layer 1 on top of the layer-0 linear-attention path.

commands.parent_prefix_probe: cd /home/lhl/amd-gpu-tuning && <OPTIMAL env flags> PYTHONPATH=nano-vllm-amd:paroquant mamba run -n therock --no-capture-output python3 /tmp/parent_prefix_probe.py
commands.guard: python3 -m compileall -q hipengine tests scripts && python3 -m pytest tests/test_qwen35_decode_state.py tests/test_qwen35_paro_layout.py tests/test_loading_materialize.py tests/test_generation_qwen35_paro.py tests/test_runtime_workspace.py tests/test_qwen35_resident_batch_layout.py tests/test_generation_batch_scheduler.py -q

blockers: Layer-0-only prefix matches parent argmax for prefill and first decode, so the earliest argmax divergence appears when layer 1 is included.
Layer 1 is a linear_attention layer; next step should compare parent and HIP hidden/logit outputs inside layer 1 linear-attention/MoE, especially accumulated state after 512 sequential prompt tokens.
No performance claim retained; this is a correctness bisect artifact.
